channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dead tree, 11th July
item.description:
March 2024 the previous mature tree was removed and a sorbus incana was planted in its place. This was a like for like replanting. It has subsequently died and needs to be removed. Please consider replacing the tree with for example a flowering cherry since the sorbus incana sheds berries and my neighbour suffered the expense of carpet replacement due to the staining caused by the tree berries. What ever your decision please do not replace with sorbus incana.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 23, Woodlea Drive, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish outside flats Bledlow close, 11th July
item.description:
Rubbish blocking pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bledlow Close, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping obstructing footpath, 11th July
item.description:
Bed mattress furniture boxes etc dumped in front of York House - now obstructing the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): York Hill, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside the Priory Tavern Pub, 11th July
item.description:
The road outside the Priory Tavern is gravelly and we have seen 2 motorcyclists skid and fall because of the road surface. We have also seen a card skid with force to the side when attempted to turn onto the road. There have been no major injuries as of yet, but as it is on a junction and by a busy pub, it is concerning that there may be more accidents and there is no sign for road users to slow down when approaching the bend, which is just before a pedestrian crossing.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kilburn Vale, Kilburn, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Packed cardboard dumped, 11th July
item.description:
Bundle of flattened cardboard dumped in alcove behind Tescos

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Long Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Food left out, 11th July
item.description:
Everyday food gets left outside attracting tons and tons of flies, the food doesn't get eaten and is a health hazard to dogs
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Whateley Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Long Street, Hackney, London

Fly tip

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Dumped cherry tree blocking pavement, 11th July
item.description:
I reported this on 25 June but it has disappeared! Unfortunately the tree hasn’t, although it has been dragged to the top of the street where it’s still causing a problem as everyone has to walk round it! These roads have v busy footfall. Please can it be sorted before someone trips over it and breaks something.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tatnell Road, Honor Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Salusbury, 11th July
item.description:
Filthy littter

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bikes blocking pavement, 11th July
item.description:
At least 30 lime bikes blocking pavement . No wheelchair pushchair pedestrian access. Photos include a person ( obscured as per your instructions) blatantly parking their bike on the pavement

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Upper Clapton Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sweeping, 11th July
item.description:
All junctions at the roundabout at James watt way/manor road there is debris on the road consisting of wood/bolts etc. . This is not good for the tyres as i saw 2 different vehicles with punctures last week. This is making the untidy and very un attractive. I have never seen a regular road sweeper carrying out a sweep of the area . This is needed on a regular basis as there are many trucks etc using manor road to the commercial sites. When was the last time a sweeper went round this area?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): James Watt Way, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wickham way hedges need drastic pruning, 11th July
item.description:
The hedges on Wickham Way are periodically cut by Council contractors. The frequency of this has been reduced. The hedges are in drastic need of cutting as they restrict parking and sight when pulling out onto Wickham Way. When will they next be cut back?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 141, Wickham Way, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree stump in ground, 11th July
item.description:
Broken tree stump sticking out could be dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Manor Way, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Over grown hedge, 11th July
item.description:
Making it difficult to use the whole pavement when using wheelchair / walking frame / pram
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodside Avenue, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Some bin bags have been left at the bottom of the stairs and the foxes have got to them, 11th July
item.description:
There keeps being rubbish bags left all over the building and then the foxes get to them and make a big mess

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Vestry Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
